briefs.title: Short-Term Rental Rules in BC Now Cover First Nation Laws — Hosts Must Comply
briefs.summary: BC updated its short-term rental rules so that laws passed by participating First Nations carry the same weight as municipal bylaws. The province also shifted certain review decisions from a 'responsible official' to a central registrar (a government official who manages the licensing registry).
briefs.action_required: Check whether a First Nation law now applies to your short-term rental property and update your listing to comply.
